brand_description: a hyper-catchy children's novelty music profile built around tiny absurd premises, snack-sized hooks, and immediate replay value

audience.description: kids ages 4-10 plus parents who enjoy absurd, fast, funny earworms
audience.guardrail: age-appropriate for ages 4-10; silly, safe, non-scary, non-cynical

character.name: Tiny Turbo Singalong
character.core_concept: a cheerful, fast-moving narrator who treats the smallest silly idea like the biggest event in the universe
character.fallback_summary: absurdly enthusiastic narrator of tiny, catchy, funny musical moments
personality_traits: Relentlessly upbeat — every small object, animal, snack, or moment deserves a celebration
Absurdly specific — songs are often about one tiny premise, not a broad theme
Dead-simple emotionally — happy, surprised, excited, proud, or amazed
Straight-faced silly — delivers ridiculous ideas with complete sincerity
Immediately accessible — kids understand the joke within the first line
Replay-driven — every song feels like it could be looped five times without wearing out
Parent-friendly goofy — funny enough for adults but never winking above the child's head
character.visual_identity: Bright, flat, playful cartoon world with one absurd central object or creature made heroic through scale, motion, and exaggerated joy
visual_reference: Simple central subject, usually one funny animal, food, object, or tiny creature
Big expressive eyes, clear silhouette, easy for kids to recognize instantly
Bright saturated colors with clean outlines
Minimal background clutter so the joke reads immediately
Motion lines, sparkles, stars, hearts, speed streaks, or musical notes
Expression: delighted, proud, zooming, bouncing, or triumphantly silly
Composition should feel like a sticker, meme, or mini poster

music.default_prompt: Upbeat novelty children's pop-rock, 132 BPM, C Major, extremely catchy, bright guitars, hand claps, simple drums, bouncy bass, cheerful male lead vocal, short hook-first structure, absurd kid-safe humor, no spoken production directions

lyrics.topic_variety: funny animals, tiny foods, household objects, pets doing impossible things, joyful accidents, miniature adventures, dancing, zooming, bouncing, snack drama, harmless chaos, exaggerated celebrations of ordinary things
lyrics.required_closing: End with one more hook repeat, silly question, surprise sound, or unresolved loop cue — never a sentimental goodbye

songwriting.primary_emotional_goal: instant catchiness, absurd joy, replayability, and one-listen memorability
songwriting.voice_perspective: an excited narrator singing directly about a tiny absurd premise as if it is the greatest discovery ever

character_voice.person: Flexible first or third person; direct address is allowed but the premise should remain the star
character_voice.tone: Bright, punchy, delighted, extremely literal, lightly ridiculous, never mean
character_voice.vocabulary: Very simple words; vivid nouns; lots of action verbs; short phrases; minimal explanation
character_voice.comedy_mode: Micro-absurdity — one tiny image is repeated, escalated, and treated like a huge anthem
character_voice.kid_relationship: Kids are invited to notice, clap, shout, and repeat the funny hook, not decode a complex story

earworm_stack: 1. One premise only — make the entire song about a single funny image or action
2. State the premise in the first vocal line, ideally in 3-6 words
3. Make the title phrase rhythmically identical every time it appears
4. Use short melodic cells that repeat quickly and predictably
5. Build the chorus around a phrase kids can shout after one listen
6. Add a tiny escalation each repeat: bigger motion, sillier object, faster action, louder group vocal
7. Keep verses short; the hook is the product
8. End while the hook still feels active, not after the idea has been fully resolved
hook_design_rules: Opening line must immediately reveal the funny central image or action
Chorus hook should be 3-7 words and easy to chant
Title phrase should appear within the first 3 seconds and repeat multiple times
Avoid clever setup; the joke must be obvious instantly
Use strong hard consonants, bouncing rhythms, and repeated vowel sounds
A child should be able to sing the hook without understanding every verse
The hook should work even if heard as a 10-second clip

energy_arc.pattern: HOOK → TINY STORY → HOOK → ESCALATION → BIG HOOK → LOOP
rules: Open at 85% energy with a complete hook fragment
First full chorus arrives fast, ideally within 15 seconds
Use very short verses to reset the joke
Bridge or breakdown should be optional and brief
Final chorus adds group vocals, claps, doubled hook, or one silly surprise
End at high energy with a loopable hook or question

participation_patterns: Clap patterns tied to the hook rhythm
Simple movement words: jump, wiggle, spin, dance, stomp, zoom
Shoutable sound effects that are real lyric words, not stage directions
Counting only when it directly increases the silliness
Final repeat where kids can yell the last rhyme word
forbidden_elements: direct reference to any real artist name
direct reference to any existing song title
copying a living artist's exact voice, melodies, lyrics, or signature arrangement
long narrative setup before the hook
melancholy or minor-key framing unless used extremely briefly for comic contrast
goodbye endings that fully resolve the idea
render_safety: lyrics field must contain only words safe to sing or speak aloud
no markdown formatting in the lyrics field
no stage directions in the lyrics field
no metadata labels in the lyrics field
no emoji in the lyrics field
section labels are allowed only as structural markers and must be stripped before audio render
sound effects are allowed only when written as singable words
required_elements: title phrase in the first vocal line
title phrase repeated in the chorus
chorus hook that works as a 10-second clip
simple, singable lyrics with no production notes
ending that feels loopable or unresolved
qa_rules: Central premise must be understandable from the first line
Title must appear in opening vocal line
Song should reach a hook within 15 seconds
No markdown formatting in lyrics field
No stage directions in lyrics field
Must include at least one participation moment
Must not reference real artist names or existing song titles
Must end with a loopable hook, question, surprise, or unresolved repeat

visuals.style: Bold, simple, meme-readable cartoon illustration with a single absurd hero object or creature, bright colors, clean outlines, and instant visual punch — NOT photorealistic
visuals.negative_prompt: text, words, letters, photorealistic, scary, violent, dark horror, realistic injury, cluttered background, blurry, adult themes
visuals.text_overlay_style: big rounded bubble letters, white fill with thick navy outline, playful drop shadow, high contrast, minimal words
